topic.what: Heterogeneous multi-agent LLM system for code vulnerability detection using game-theoretic design
topic.how: 3+1 architecture: 3 parallel cloud experts (DeepSeek-V3) + 1 local verifier (Qwen3-8B) with two-layer game framework (cooperative expert coalition + adversarial verification)
topic.why_matters: Enables cost-effective vulnerability detection ($0.002/sample) with game-theoretic guarantees; addresses trade-off between detection accuracy and computational cost

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of detected vulnerabilities that are real
characteristics.context: Adversarial verifier significantly reduces false positives
All configurations achieve 100% recall; verifier improves precision not recall

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of benign samples incorrectly flagged
characteristics.context: Verifier filters 41 additional false positives from 119 to 78
Moderate positive correlation; verifier substantially improves discriminative power

characteristics.what_itasures: API cost per code sample analyzed
characteristics.vs_baseline: 3x cost of single expert ($0.0006) for significantly better F1
characteristics.context: Enables $1 per 500 functions for CI/CD integration

characteristics.what_it_measures: Parallel vs serial expert execution time ratio
characteristics.context: Parallel execution reduces expert time 3x with no quality degradation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Accuracy of CWE type identification for detected vulnerabilities
characteristics.context: Coalition achieves correct CWE classification for all true positives
Verifier dominates latency; optimizing verifier inference would yield improvements

requires: NIST Juliet or similar benchmark datasets
Cloud API budget for expert agents

novelty: Two-layer game framework formalizing cooperative expert coalition and adversarial verification
Heterogeneous cloud-local architecture optimizing cost-quality trade-offs
Game-theoretic predictions validated empirically (super-additivity, precision improvement, mechanism design)
Zero-marginal-cost local verifier achieving significant precision gains
open_problems: Evaluation limited to synthetic Juliet dataset; real-world generalization untested
High FPR (60%) on benign samples remains substantial challenge
State-of-the-art approaches achieve only ~35% F1 on PrimeVul benchmark
Iterative expert-verifier dialogue not fully explored
Dynamic agent selection based on code characteristics not implemented
